text.title: Voice Implementation Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the production voice-agent implementation. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Replace energy-based VAD with Silero VAD or WebRTC VAD for any production deployment outside a silent recording booth.
rule.rationale: Energy/RMS-threshold VAD triggers on HVAC, keyboard, music. Silero/WebRTC use learned speech models that work in real environments.

rule.statement: Run blocking tool functions in a thread executor (`asyncio.to_thread` / `run_in_executor`); never call them on the event loop directly.
rule.rationale: Sync tool functions block the loop — no audio is sent or received during the blocking call, the user hears dead air.

rule.statement: Strip markdown from LLM responses before TTS: `re.sub(r'[*_`#>]', '', text)`. Never feed raw LLM output to TTS.
rule.rationale: TTS reads markdown literally — "**bold**" becomes "asterisk asterisk bold asterisk asterisk".

rule.statement: Apply sliding window to `conversation_history`: keep system prompt + last N turns + relevant tool results. Cap `max_response_tokens` at 150–200.
rule.rationale: Voice responses >200 tokens take too long to TTS. Unbounded history overflows context at ~50 turns and silently truncates.

rule.statement: Use OpenAI Realtime API for deployments requiring <300ms end-to-end latency. STT+LLM+TTS chain minimum is 650–1900ms.
rule.rationale: Native WebSocket protocol bypasses serial pipeline stages; chain latency is structural.
Without per-turn logs, debugging tool failures or truncation requires replaying the exact conversation — usually impossible.

rule.statement: Handle `WebSocketDisconnect` separately from generic `Exception` in the WS endpoint; never wrap both in bare `except Exception`.
rule.rationale: Disconnect is expected, errors are not. Conflating them suppresses real failures and pages the on-call for normal client exits.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire the production voice agent per procedure.
No conversational loop required or batch-only audio → use `speech-to-text-basics` + `tts-implementation` directly.
